summary: Wonderful tape job captures a vibrant Scarlet > Fire combo and strong first set, though the second set isn’t quite as tight.
review: The recording is praised as a “wonderful tape job” with clear mix that highlights Keith’s keyboards and Phil’s rhythmic soloing. Musically the show shines on the bouncy Scarlet > Fire sequence and the SOTM > Sugar Magnolia closer, while the second set is noted as slightly weaker and Jerry’s brief absence during Fire detracts a bit.

ai_rating.rationale: Average reviewer rating is 4.33/5; most comments praise both performance and tape quality, with only minor criticisms about the second set and a brief guitar silence.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Tape job described as wonderful and clear; mix brings out keyboards and rhythm section nicely. No major drop‑outs or muddiness reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is energetic and well‑played; the second set, while still enjoyable, is considered less tight than the opener.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Absent for ~90 seconds during 'Scarlet > Fire', which some reviewers saw as a drawback.
band_member_comments.Phil: His bass (and talking drum) solo is highlighted as an interesting rhythmic feature.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s playing receives enthusiastic praise, especially on the Scarlet > Fire combo.
